title: centaurea cineraria subsp. cinerea
intro: Centaurea cineraria subsp. cinerea, commonly known as silvery lupine or blue lupine, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Centaurea cineraria subsp. cinerea include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its efficacy and safety as a treatment for various ailments require further scientific research.
